answer:
To calculate sensitivity, you need the true positive rate, which requires knowing the number of false negatives—men with prostate cancer who tested negative. Since only men with PSA >5 ng/mL had biopsies, you lack data on those with PSA ≤5 ng/mL who might have cancer. Therefore, you need prostate biopsies of men with test results equal to or below 5 ng/mL to determine false negatives.

\boxed{D}
